summary: Verified post-login redirect bug fix. AuthCallback now uses window.location.replace('/dashboard') and AuthContext skips /auth/me when hash has session_id. All 4 UI redirect scenarios pass.

tests_executed.name: Invalid session_id in hash -> redirects to / (login)
Valid session_token cookie + visit /dashboard -> Dashboard renders (Command Center visible, QA Tester user shown)
Valid session_token cookie + visit / -> auto-redirects to /dashboard
No cookie + visit / -> Login page with google-login-button

critical_code_review_comments: AuthCallback correctly uses hard redirect (window.location.replace) to avoid react-router v7 hash retention issues.
AuthContext.useEffect properly skips /auth/me when hash contains session_id, preventing race with cookie exchange.
Console shows 401s on initial /auth/me when logged out — expected behavior but could be silenced by checking for cookie presence first (minor).
test_credentials: session_token cookie = test_session_karya1 (user qa.karya@example.com) — set via Playwright context cookie
context_for_next_testing_agent: Post-login redirect flow (Login -> Dashboard) is verified working via cookie injection. Actual OAuth exchange cannot be tested E2E because test_session_karya1 is a DB-persisted token, not an Emergent OAuth X-Session-ID.
rca of the issue: Previous AuthCallback used setUser + history.replaceState + navigate('/dashboard'); with react-router v7 the hash/router state race caused user to remain on Login. Fix: window.location.replace('/dashboard') forces a full reload on a clean URL, and AuthContext skips /auth/me while hash contains session_id so the callback owns the exchange. Verified: with valid cookie, /dashboard renders Dashboard and / redirects to /dashboard; with invalid session_id in hash, user lands on Login.
